metadata.title: The NATO Exercise That Triggered Nuclear Alert
metadata.global_visual_style: Kodachrome 60s–80s film grain, cinematic noir lighting, historical archival photo style, high-contrast shadows, desaturated cold tones with selective amber highlights. 9:16 vertical framing. Fast cuts — visual change every 3 seconds minimum.
metadata.bg_music_note: No background music. Intentional — maximizes Shorts revenue pool allocation. All audio is narration and SFX only.

timeline.narration: In 1983, NATO ran a military exercise so realistic, the Soviet Union began preparing a nuclear strike to stop it.
timeline.visual_description: Black screen. Large white sans-serif text snaps in: 'IT WAS JUST AN EXERCISE.' Cold, stark. 9:16 vertical frame.
timeline.sfx_prompt: complete silence, then distant alarm tone rising

timeline.narration: November 1983. Deep inside a hardened bunker in East Germany, Soviet Air Force commanders receive an order never issued in peacetime.
timeline.visual_description: Extreme close-up of a Soviet officer's hand reaching for a red telephone receiver in a concrete command bunker. Harsh fluorescent light, cold shadow. Kodachrome 80s film grain, cinematic noir.
timeline.sfx_prompt: heavy bunker door locking, distant electrical hum

timeline.narration: Nuclear warheads are to be loaded onto combat aircraft. Immediately.
timeline.visual_description: Low-angle wide shot of Soviet Tupolev bombers lined on a dark East German airfield tarmac at night. Ground crew in shadows, arc lights cutting through freezing fog. Historical archival photo style, high contrast.
timeline.sfx_prompt: aircraft engines spooling up, boots on wet tarmac

timeline.narration: Seventy-five mobile nuclear missile launchers vanish into the forests of Eastern Europe. A Soviet submarine slides under the Arctic ice.
timeline.visual_description: Aerial view of dark pine forest, barely visible camouflage netting concealing missile transporter vehicles beneath. Cold blue moonlight through snow-dusted branches. Kodachrome grain, desaturated greens.
timeline.sfx_prompt: submarine sonar ping fading to silence, wind through frozen trees

timeline.narration: Eleven thousand nuclear warheads are placed on maximum combat alert.
timeline.visual_description: Split-frame: left — NATO officers laughing over a map in a lit Brussels command room; right — Soviet generals in grim silence studying readiness reports. Hard chiaroscuro, Kodachrome grain.

timeline.narration: NATO had absolutely no idea any of this was happening. They thought they were running a training drill.
timeline.visual_description: Declassified NATO exercise document stamped TOP SECRET — ABLE ARCHER 83. Cold white light, sharp shadows, Kodachrome film grain texture overlay.

timeline.narration: Able Archer 83 was the most realistic war simulation NATO had ever conducted. New communication protocols. Realistic radio silence. Simulated heads-of-state involvement.
timeline.visual_description: NATO command center interior, 1983 — officers at terminals, maps on wall displays, headsets on. Warm amber operational lighting against cold grey walls. Historical archival photo style, Kodachrome grain.

timeline.narration: The Soviets, already convinced the US was planning a first strike, saw Able Archer as the cover story for the real thing.
timeline.visual_description: Soviet intelligence analysis room, officers in uniform bent over decoded intercepts and maps of Western Europe. Single overhead lamp, deep shadows. Cinematic noir, Kodachrome 80s grain.
timeline.sfx_prompt: urgent murmuring in Russian, paper turning

timeline.narration: The only person who knew both sides of this was a British double agent inside the KGB — Oleg Gordievsky.
timeline.visual_description: Silhouetted figure in a trench coat at a rainy London street corner at dusk, collar turned up. A black cab passing reflects wet light. Cinematic noir, Kodachrome grain.
timeline.sfx_prompt: London rain, distant traffic, quiet footsteps

timeline.narration: Gordievsky passed the alarm to British intelligence. Margaret Thatcher passed it to Reagan. What Reagan learned genuinely shocked him.
timeline.visual_description: Split archival: Thatcher left, Reagan right — both at separate official desks, serious expressions, papers in hand. Historical archival photo style, Kodachrome warm tone.

timeline.narration: Reagan had not understood that the Soviets genuinely believed the United States might strike first. He thought the fear was theater.
timeline.visual_description: Reagan at his Oval Office desk, reading a classified briefing document, expression shifting — from confidence to something resembling genuine unease. Archival photo style, warm Washington light. Kodachrome grain.

timeline.narration: Able Archer ended on November 11th. The warheads were unloaded. The war that almost happened by accident quietly stood down.
timeline.visual_description: Soviet bomber on the tarmac, ground crew removing equipment — nuclear warheads being returned to storage. Night scene, arc lights, exhaled relief in postures. Kodachrome grain, cinematic noir.
timeline.sfx_prompt: aircraft engines powering down, cargo equipment sounds, quiet returning

timeline.narration: The full 20-minute documentary is on the channel. Everything NATO didn't know it nearly started.
timeline.visual_description: Channel-branded card — the Able Archer 83 long-form documentary thumbnail with bold text overlay: 'FULL STORY: 20 MINUTES'. Chronicles of Eras channel name and logo. Dark background.
LOOP SETUP — identical to opening frame: Black screen. White text: 'IT WAS JUST AN EXERCISE.' Holds for 3 seconds. Seamless replay trigger.
